result.summary: Contentful exposes multiple developer surfaces: REST APIs for delivery, preview, management, images, SCIM, user management, and personalization experience data; a GraphQL content API; hosted MCP endpoints (global and EU); and the Contentful CLI.
result.description: Contentful is a content platform for creating, managing, and delivering structured content across websites, apps, and other digital experiences. It also offers related capabilities for preview, user provisioning, and personalization.

delivery_api_token.label: Content Delivery API access token
delivery_api_token.setup: In the Contentful web app, open your target space, then go to `Settings` → `API keys` and create or view an API key for that space/environment. Use its delivery access token with the Content Delivery API. See [Authentication](https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/references/authentication) and the [Content Delivery API overview](https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/references/content-delivery-api/overview).

cma_pat.label: Content Management API personal access token
cma_pat.setup: Create a personal access token in the Contentful web app at [Account settings → API Tokens → Personal Access Tokens](https://app.contentful.com/account/profile/cma_tokens). The docs also describe generating a management token from [Login](https://be.contentful.com/login) via `Settings > API keys > Content management tokens > Generate personal token`. See [Authentication](https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/references/authentication), [SCIM API overview](https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/references/scim-api/overview), and [CLI authentication](https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/tutorials/cli/authentication).

contentful_oauth.label: Contentful user OAuth sign-in for remote MCP
contentful_oauth.setup: Use an MCP client that supports OAuth and connect it to the hosted endpoint. The client will redirect you to sign in with your Contentful account (`be.contentful.com` for global or `be.eu.contentful.com` for EU) and authorize the session. The remote server also requires the Contentful MCP app to be installed in each target space/environment. See [Model Context Protocol (MCP) server](https://www.contentful.com/developers/docs/tools/mcp-server).

requiredHeaders.name: Content-Type
requiredHeaders.description: Required on all SCIM API requests.
